== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural approval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were appointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate Appointments) Higher Normal School graduates were appointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were appointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * May 26: Curriculum for male students. * May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. * June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: * April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. * October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.

revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily appointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily appointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural approval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were appointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate Appointments) Higher Normal School graduates were appointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were appointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * May 26: Curriculum for male students. * May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. * June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: * April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. * October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.

== See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural approval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were appointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate Appointments) Higher Normal School graduates were appointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were appointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * May 26: Curriculum for male students. * May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. * June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: * April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. * October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily appointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily appointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural approval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were appointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate Appointments) Higher Normal School graduates were appointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were appointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * May 26: Curriculum for male students. * May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. * June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: * April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. * October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.

== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural aqqroval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were aqqointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate Aqqointments) Higher Normal School graduates were aqqointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were aqqointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * May 26: Curriculum for male students. * May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. * June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: * April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. * October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily aqqointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily aqqointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural aqqroval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were aqqointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate Aqqointments) Higher Normal School graduates were aqqointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were aqqointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * May 26: Curriculum for male students. * May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. * June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: * April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. * October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily aqqointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily aqqointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural aqqroval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were aqqointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate Aqqointments) Higher Normal School graduates were aqqointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were aqqointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * May 26: Curriculum for male students. * May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. * June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: * October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: * April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. * October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily aqqointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily aqqointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.

=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.

'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]
{{Education-stub}} {{Gakkōrei}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 6]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]
{{Education-stub}} {{Gakkōrei}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

revision.comment: 53 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:師範学校令 up to rev 95217809
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2023年5月}} {{日本の法令 | 題名=師範学校令 | 効力=廃止 | 種類=[[教育法]] | 所管=[[文部省]] | 内容=[[師範学校]]の規定 | 関連=[[師範教育令]] | リンク=[{{NDLDC|2944042/1}} 官報 1886年4月10日] | ウィキソース=師範学校令 }} '''師範学校令'''（しはんがっこうれい、明治19年4月10日勅令第13号）は、[[1886年]]（[[明治]]19年）[[4月10日]]に[[公布]]された[[勅令]]であり、いわゆる「[[学校令]]」の一つである。[[教員]]を養成する[[学校]]（[[師範学校]]）に関して定めている。
== 概要 == === 公布 === 1886年（明治19年）4月10日（明治19年勅令第13号） [[総理大臣]]:[[伊藤博文]]（[[第1次伊藤内閣]]）／[[文部大臣]]:[[森有礼]]
同日に[[小学校令]]（明治19年勅令第14号）・[[中学校令]]（明治19年勅令第15号）も公布された。
=== ポイント === [[師範学校]]を[[師範学校#高等師範学校・女子高等師範学校|高等師範学校]]と[[師範学校#尋常師範学校|尋常師範学校]]の2種類に分ける。
; 高等師範学校 :* '''東京に1箇所'''設置しなければならない。 :* これに伴い、東京師範学校が[[東京高等師範学校|高等師範学校]]（のち、東京高等師範学校に改称。現・[[筑波大学]]）となり、東京女子師範学校が高等師範学校の女子師範学科となる。なお、師範学校令公布の4年後[[1890年]]（明治23年）に女子師範学科は分離し「[[東京女子高等師範学校|女子高等師範学校]]」（[[お茶の水女子大学]]の前身）として独立した。 :* 経費は'''[[国庫]]'''から支払われる。 :* 生徒は卒業後、原則として'''尋常師範学校の校長および教員'''に任命される。 : ; 尋常師範学校 :* '''各府県に1箇所'''設置しなければならない。 :* それまでの師範学校の初等師範学科・中等師範学科・高等師範学'''科'''<ref>「師範学校教則大綱」（1881年（明治14年）[[8月19日]]達第29号）の第7条で「卒業後、初等師範学科では小学校初等科の教員に、中等師範学科では小学校初等科または中等科の教員に、高等師範学科では小学校初等科、中等科、または高等科の教員になることができる」と卒業後の資格が規定されていた。</ref>が尋常師範学校となる。 :* 経費は'''[[地方税]]'''から支払われる。 :* 生徒は卒業後、原則として'''公立小学校の校長および教員'''に任命される。
生徒の'''学資は学校が支給'''することになっていた。そのため、進学を希望している者の中で学資が支給されない学校に入学しても支払いが見込めない（いわゆる、経済的に恵まれない）者は他の学校より師範学校に入学することが多かった。
=== 一部改正 === * 師範学校令中追加ノ件 （明治20年勅令第30号） * 師範学校令中改正ノ件 （明治24年勅令第216号）
=== 廃止 === [[1898年]]（明治31年）[[4月1日]] [[師範教育令]]（明治30年10月9日勅令第346号）の施行により廃止。
== 内容 == ; 第1条（目的） : 師範学校は教員となるべき者を養成する所とする。ただし生徒に順良・信愛・威重の気質を備えさせることに注目すべき者とする。 ; 第2条（種類） : 師範学校を高等と尋常の二等に分ける。高等師範学校は[[文部大臣]]の管理に属する。 ; 第3条（設置数） : 高等師範学校は東京に1箇所、尋常師範学校は府県に1箇所ずつ設置しなければならない。 ; 第4条（経費） : 高等師範学校の経費は国庫より、尋常師範学校の経費は地方税から支出しなければならない。 ; 第5条（尋常師範学校の経費） : 尋常師範学校の経費に必要な地方税の額は府知事[[県知事|県令]]がその予算を調整し、文部大臣の認可を受けなければならない。 ; 第6条（教員の任期） : 師範学校長および教員の任期は5年とする。5年以上継続してもよい。 ; 第7条（尋常師範学校長の兼務） : 尋常師範学校長はその府県の学務課長を兼務することができる。 ; 第8条（生徒募集） : 師範学校生徒の募集および卒業後の服務に関する規則は文部大臣の定めるところによる。 ; 第9条（生徒の学資） : 師範学校生徒の学資（学費）はその学校から支給しなければならない。 ; 第10条（高等師範学校卒業時の資格） : 高等師範学校の卒業生を尋常師範学校長および教員に任命することとする。ただし場合によっては各種の学校長および教員に任命することができる。 ; 第11条（尋常師範学校卒業時の資格） : 尋常師範学校の卒業生を公立小学校長および教員に任命することとする。ただし場合によっては各種の学校長および教員に任命することができる。 ; 第12条（学科・教科書） : 師範学校の学科およびその程度ならびに教科書は文部大臣の定めるところによる。
== 関連規則・規定 == === 尋常師範学校 === * 1886年（明治19年） ** [[5月26日]] - 「尋常師範学校ノ学科及其程度」（男子生徒について） ** [[5月28日]] - 「生徒募集規則及卒業生服務規則」 *** 尋常師範学校の入学資格を[[高等小学校]]卒業以上の学力を有し'''17歳以上20歳以下'''の者、修業年限を'''4年'''と規程。 ** [[6月4日]] - 「尋常師範学校男生徒の学資支給に関する件」 * [[1888年]]（明治21年）[[8月21日]] - 「尋常師範学校設備準則」 * [[1889年]]（明治22年）10月 - 「尋常師範学校ノ女生徒ニ課スヘキ学科及其程度」 * [[1892年]]（明治25年）[[7月11日]] ** 「尋常師範学校ノ学科及其程度」（改定、女子に関する規程も包括） ** 「尋常師範学校生徒定員規則」（改定） ** 「生徒募集規則及卒業生服務規則」（改定） ** 「尋常師範学校設備規則」 ** 「尋常師範学校簡易科規程」- 就学児童の増加に伴う教員の不足を補うために簡易科を設置。修業年限は2年4ヶ月で、生徒は男子のみに限る。
=== 高等師範学校 === * 1886年（明治19年）[[10月14日]] - 「高等師範学校ノ学科及其程度」、「高等師範学校生徒募集規則」、「高等師範学校卒業生服務規則」 ** 男子師範学科の入学資格を尋常師範学校を卒業した者、修業年限を3年と規定。 ** 女子師範学科は尋常師範学校第2学年修了者を入学させ、修業年限を4年と規定。 * [[1894年]]（明治27年） ** [[4月6日]] - 「高等師範学校規程」 *** 従来の3学科（理化学科・博物学科・文学科）を、文科・理科の2学科に改める。 *** [[旧制中等教育学校|中等学校]]教員の養成を拡充する目的で研究科・専修科・選科を設置。 ** [[10月2日]] - 「女子高等師範学校規程」- 学科を尋常師範学校女子部の課程に比べさらに精深な程度の教授を行なう学校とする。
== 作品 == [[夏目漱石]]『[[坊っちゃん]]』では、[[旧制中学校|（旧制）中学生]]たちと師範学校生たちとの喧嘩のシーンがある。そこで「〔略〕（中学生たちの）前の方にいる連中は、しきりに何だ地方税の癖に、引き込めと、怒鳴ってる」といった師範学校令第4条に関係する記述が見られる。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[師範学校]]
== 外部リンク == {{Wikisource|師範学校令}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm 師範学校令] - 文部科学省
{{Education-stub}} {{学校令}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:しはんかつこうれい}} [[Category:廃止された日本の勅令]] [[Category:日本の旧制教育機関の法令]] [[Category:日本の師範学校|令]] [[Category:日本の高等師範学校|令]] [[Category:明治時代の教育]] [[Category:日本の師範教育機関|法しはんかつこうれい]] [[Category:日本の教員養成の歴史]] [[Category:1886年の法]] [[Category:1886年の日本]] [[Category:1886年4月]] [[Category:1898年に廃止された法]]

=== 一部改正 === * 師範学校令中追加ノ件 （明治20年勅令第30号） * 師範学校令中改正ノ件 （明治24年勅令第216号）
=== 廃止 === [[1898年]]（明治31年）[[4月1日]] [[師範教育令]]（明治30年10月9日勅令第346号）の施行により廃止。
== 内容 == ; 第1条（目的） : 師範学校は教員となるべき者を養成する所とする。ただし生徒に順良・信愛・威重の気質を備えさせることに注目すべき者とする。 ; 第2条（種類） : 師範学校を高等と尋常の二等に分ける。高等師範学校は[[文部大臣]]の管理に属する。 ; 第3条（設置数） : 高等師範学校は東京に1箇所、尋常師範学校は府県に1箇所ずつ設置しなければならない。 ; 第4条（経費） : 高等師範学校の経費は国庫より、尋常師範学校の経費は地方税から支出しなければならない。 ; 第5条（尋常師範学校の経費） : 尋常師範学校の経費に必要な地方税の額は府知事[[県知事|県令]]がその予算を調整し、文部大臣の認可を受けなければならない。 ; 第6条（教員の任期） : 師範学校長および教員の任期は5年とする。5年以上継続してもよい。 ; 第7条（尋常師範学校長の兼務） : 尋常師範学校長はその府県の学務課長を兼務することができる。 ; 第8条（生徒募集） : 師範学校生徒の募集および卒業後の服務に関する規則は文部大臣の定めるところによる。 ; 第9条（生徒の学資） : 師範学校生徒の学資（学費）はその学校から支給しなければならない。 ; 第10条（高等師範学校卒業時の資格） : 高等師範学校の卒業生を尋常師範学校長および教員に任命することとする。ただし場合によっては各種の学校長および教員に任命することができる。 ; 第11条（尋常師範学校卒業時の資格） : 尋常師範学校の卒業生を公立小学校長および教員に任命することとする。ただし場合によっては各種の学校長および教員に任命することができる。 ; 第12条（学科・教科書） : 師範学校の学科およびその程度ならびに教科書は文部大臣の定めるところによる。
== 関連規則・規定 == === 尋常師範学校 === * 1886年（明治19年） ** [[5月26日]] - 「尋常師範学校ノ学科及其程度」（男子生徒について） ** [[5月28日]] - 「生徒募集規則及卒業生服務規則」 *** 尋常師範学校の入学資格を[[高等小学校]]卒業以上の学力を有し'''17歳以上20歳以下'''の者、修業年限を'''4年'''と規程。 ** [[6月4日]] - 「尋常師範学校男生徒の学資支給に関する件」 * [[1888年]]（明治21年）[[8月21日]] - 「尋常師範学校設備準則」 * [[1889年]]（明治22年）10月 - 「尋常師範学校ノ女生徒ニ課スヘキ学科及其程度」 * [[1892年]]（明治25年）[[7月11日]] ** 「尋常師範学校ノ学科及其程度」（改定、女子に関する規程も包括） ** 「尋常師範学校生徒定員規則」（改定） ** 「生徒募集規則及卒業生服務規則」（改定） ** 「尋常師範学校設備規則」 ** 「尋常師範学校簡易科規程」- 就学児童の増加に伴う教員の不足を補うために簡易科を設置。修業年限は2年4ヶ月で、生徒は男子のみに限る。
=== 高等師範学校 === * 1886年（明治19年）[[10月14日]] - 「高等師範学校ノ学科及其程度」、「高等師範学校生徒募集規則」、「高等師範学校卒業生服務規則」 ** 男子師範学科の入学資格を尋常師範学校を卒業した者、修業年限を3年と規定。 ** 女子師範学科は尋常師範学校第2学年修了者を入学させ、修業年限を4年と規定。 * [[1894年]]（明治27年） ** [[4月6日]] - 「高等師範学校規程」 *** 従来の3学科（理化学科・博物学科・文学科）を、文科・理科の2学科に改める。 *** [[旧制中等教育学校|中等学校]]教員の養成を拡充する目的で研究科・専修科・選科を設置。 ** [[10月2日]] - 「女子高等師範学校規程」- 学科を尋常師範学校女子部の課程に比べさらに精深な程度の教授を行なう学校とする。

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.

=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.

Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.

== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]
