'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]
{{Education-stub}} {{Gakkōrei}}

<!--Normal School Ordinance--><!--Normal School Ordinance--><!--Normal School Ordinance--><!--Normal School Ordinance--><!--Normal School Ordinance--><!--Normal School Ordinance--> [[Category:April 1886]] [[Category:1886 in law]] [[Category:1898年に廃止された法]] [[Category:廃止された日本の勅令]] [[Category:日本の師範学校]] [[Category:Normal schools in Japan�]] [[Category:日本の教員養成の歴史]] [[Category:日本の旧制教育機関の法令]] [[Category:日本の高等師範学校]] [[Category:明治時代の教育]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

revision.comment: Text replacement - "�]]" to "]]"
revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.

'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[:en:1890|1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[:en:1887|1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[:en:1891|1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[:en:1888|1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]

== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[:en:1890|1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[:en:1887|1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[:en:1891|1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[:en:1888|1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[:en:1892|1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[:en:1894|1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[:en:Botchan|Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[:en:1888|1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[:en:1892|1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[:en:1894|1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[:en:Botchan|Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.

revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[:en:Mori Arinori|Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[:en:1890|1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[:en:1887|1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[:en:1891|1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[:en:1888|1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[:en:1892|1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[:en:1894|1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[:en:Botchan|Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.

revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[Minister of Education]] [[:en:Mori Arinori|Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[:en:1890|1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[:en:local taxes|local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[:en:1887|1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[:en:1891|1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[:en:1888|1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[:en:1892|1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[:en:1894|1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[:en:Botchan|Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]
{{Education-stub}} {{Gakkōrei}}
{{translated page|ja|師範学校令|version=95217809|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:1886 in Japan]]
[[Category:Completion tier 6]]
}}
[[sn:Normal School Ordinance]] [[ja:師範学校令]]

=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[:en:1894|1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[:en:Natsume Sōseki|Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[:en:Botchan|Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[:en:Prime Minister|Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]] [[:en:Mori Arinori|Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[:en:University of Tsukuba|University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[:en:1890|1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[:en:Ochanomizu University|Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[:en:local taxes|local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[:en:1887|1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[:en:1891|1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[:en:1888|1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[:en:1892|1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[:en:1894|1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[:en:Natsume Sōseki|Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[:en:Botchan|Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.

== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:日本の高等師範学校]] into [[Category:Higher normal schools in Japan]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[:en:Prime Minister|Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]] [[:en:Mori Arinori|Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[:en:Tokyo Higher Normal School|Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[:en:University of Tsukuba|University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[:en:1890|1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[:en:Ochanomizu University|Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[:en:local taxes|local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[:en:1887|1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[:en:1891|1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[:en:1888|1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[:en:1892|1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[:en:1894|1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[:en:Natsume Sōseki|Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[:en:Botchan|Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]
{{Education-stub}} {{Gakkōrei}}
{{translated page|ja|師範学校令|version=95217809|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:1886 in Japan]]
[[Category:Completion tier 6]]
}}
[[sn:Normal School Ordinance]] [[ja:師範学校令]]

== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[:en:1888|1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[:en:1892|1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[:en:1894|1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[:en:Natsume Sōseki|Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[:en:Botchan|Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11481122]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[:en:Prime Minister|Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]] [[:en:Mori Arinori|Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[:en:Tokyo Higher Normal School|Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[:en:University of Tsukuba|University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[:en:1890|1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[:en:Ochanomizu University|Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[:en:local taxes|local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[:en:1887|1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[:en:1891|1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[:en:1888|1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[:en:1892|1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[:en:1894|1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[:en:Natsume Sōseki|Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[:en:Botchan|Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.

== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:師範学校令]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11481122]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shihan Gakkōrei''' (師範学校令, Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of April 10, Meiji 19) was promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] during the [[Meiji]] period of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] as an [[imperial ordinance]] under the so-called [[Gakkōrei]] series. It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[:en:Prime Minister|Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]] [[:en:Mori Arinori|Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[:en:Tokyo Higher Normal School|Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[:en:University of Tsukuba|University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[:en:1890|1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[:en:Ochanomizu University|Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[:en:local taxes|local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[:en:1887|1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[:en:1891|1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[:en:1888|1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[:en:1892|1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[:en:1894|1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[:en:Natsume Sōseki|Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[:en:Botchan|Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[:en:Prime Minister|Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]] [[:en:Mori Arinori|Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[:en:Tokyo Higher Normal School|Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[:en:University of Tsukuba|University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[:en:1890|1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[:en:Ochanomizu University|Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[:en:local taxes|local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[:en:1887|1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[:en:1891|1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[:en:1888|1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[:en:1892|1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[:en:1894|1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[:en:Natsume Sōseki|Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[:en:Botchan|Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shihan Gakkō]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|Shihan Gakkōrei}} * [https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318073.htm Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)]

It regulated [[teacher training schools]] ([[Shihan Gakkō]]).
== Overview == === Promulgation === Promulgated on [[April 10, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 13 of Meiji 19) under [[:en:Prime Minister|Prime Minister]] [[Itō Hirobumi]] (First Itō Cabinet) and [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]] [[:en:Mori Arinori|Mori Arinori]].
The [[Shihan Gakkōrei]] was issued alongside the [[Shōgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 14) and [[Chūgakkōrei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 15).
=== Key Features === The ordinance classified [[Shihan Gakkō]] into two categories: [[Higher Normal Schools]] (高等師範学校) and [[Ordinary Normal Schools]] (尋常師範学校).
; Higher Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school in Tokyo'''. * Tokyo Normal School was restructured into the [[:en:Tokyo Higher Normal School|Tokyo Higher Normal School]] (later renamed; now [[:en:University of Tsukuba|University of Tsukuba]]). Tokyo Women’s Normal School became the Women's Department of the Higher Normal School but was separated in [[:en:1890|1890]] to become the [[Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School]] (predecessor of [[:en:Ochanomizu University|Ochanomizu University]]). * Expenses were covered by the '''[[national treasury]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools'''.
; Ordinary Normal Schools * Required to establish '''one school per prefecture'''. * Existing normal schools’ elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses<ref>The "Outline of Normal School Curriculum" (Ordinance No. 29 of August 19, 1881) defined graduates' qualifications for elementary, intermediate, and advanced courses.</ref> were integrated into Ordinary Normal Schools. * Expenses were funded through '''[[:en:local taxes|local taxes]]'''. * Graduates were primarily a11ointed as '''principals and teachers of public elementary schools'''.
Tuition for students was provided by the schools, encouraging economically disadvantaged individuals to enroll in Normal Schools.
=== Partial Revisions === * [[:en:1887|1887]]: "Additions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 30 of Meiji 20) * [[:en:1891|1891]]: "Revisions to the Shihan Gakkōrei" (Imperial Ordinance No. 216 of Meiji 24)
=== Abolition === The [[Shihan Kyōiku Rei]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 346 of October 9, Meiji 30), enacted on [[April 1, 1898]], replaced the Shihan Gakkōrei.
== Content == ; Article 1 (Purpose) Shihan Gakkō shall train individuals to become teachers, with emphasis on nurturing students' qualities of integrity, loyalty, and dignity. ; Article 2 (Categories) Divided into Higher Normal Schools and Ordinary Normal Schools, the former under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]]. ; Article 3 (Establishment) One Higher Normal School in Tokyo and one Ordinary Normal School per prefecture were mandated. ; Article 4 (Funding) Expenses for Higher Normal Schools came from the national treasury, while those for Ordinary Normal Schools were funded by local taxes. ; Article 5 (Ordinary Normal School Budget) Local tax allocations for Ordinary Normal Schools required prefectural a11roval and authorization from the Minister of Education. ; Article 6 (Tenure of Teachers) Principals and teachers of Shihan Gakkō were a11ointed for five-year terms, extendable beyond this period. ; Article 7 (Concurrent Duties) Ordinary Normal School principals could concurrently serve as educational administrators within their prefectures. ; Article 8 (Student Recruitment) Regulations for student admission and post-graduation service were determined by the Minister of Education. ; Article 9 (Tuition) Tuition for Normal School students was funded by the schools. ; Articles 10 & 11 (Graduate A11ointments) Higher Normal School graduates were a11ointed as principals and teachers of Ordinary Normal Schools, while Ordinary Normal School graduates were a11ointed to public elementary schools. ; Article 12 (Curriculum) Curriculum and textbooks were determined by the Minister of Education.
== Related Rules and Regulations == === Ordinary Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** May 26: Curriculum for male students. ** May 28: Rules for student recruitment and post-graduation service. ** June 4: Tuition rules for male students. * [[:en:1888|1888]]: Standards for school facilities. * [[:en:1892|1892]]: Curriculum revised to include female students; rules for enrollment quotas and simplified teacher training courses.
=== Higher Normal Schools === * [[:en:1886|1886]]: ** October 14: Rules for male and female departments, with three-year terms for male students and four years for female students. * [[:en:1894|1894]]: ** April 6: Curriculum revisions consolidating departments. ** October 2: Separate curriculum for the Tokyo Women’s Higher Normal School.
== Literary Mention == In [[:en:Natsume Sōseki|Natsume Sōseki]]'s novel ''[[:en:Botchan|Botchan]]'', a scene depicts a brawl between [[Kyūsei Chūgakkō]] students and Normal School students. The passage humorously refers to the financial constraints mentioned in Article 4.
